title: `podman pod create` command fails sometimes due to k8s.gcr.io
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**
`podman pod create` command fails inside a RHEL/CentOS/archlinux VM guest

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Spin up a VM (centos-stream or rhel8 or archlinux - your choice) 

2. Install latest podman inside the VM

3. Run `podman pod  create -n sample`

**Describe the results you received:**
```
[root@archlinux ~]# podman pod create -n sample
ERRO[0006] Error freeing pod lock after failed creation: no such file or directory
Error: error adding Infra Container: Error writing blob: error storing blob to file "/var/tmp/storage507709670/1": error happened during read: unexpected EOF
```


**Describe the results you expected:**

Expected it to create the pod

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

Strangely, it works all of a sudden sometimes. I don't know why. Very fickle. Majority of the times, it doesn't work. If so, why? Does this possibly have something to do with k8s.gcr.io/pause repository? Weird. This command is extremely unstable. It only works rarely. I couldn't figure out a pattern when and why it works if it does.

Here's a very similar issue:

```
[root@archlinux ~]# podman pull k8s.gcr.io/pause
Trying to pull k8s.gcr.io/pause:latest...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob a3ed95caeb02 done
Copying blob a3ed95caeb02 done
Copying blob 4964c72cd024 [====================================>-] 66.7KiB / 68.0KiB
  unexpected EOF
Error: Error writing blob: error storing blob to file "/var/tmp/storage387175294/3": error happened during read: unexpected EOF
[root@archlinux ~]#
```

So, does `podman pod create` has something to do with the above issue?

I am not sure if this is happening due to lack of disk space either because, as per below, there's plenty of available disk space (19GB) inside the VM mounted on `/`.

```
[root@archlinux ~]# df -h
Filesystem      Size  Used Avail Use% Mounted on
dev             1.5G     0  1.5G   0% /dev
run             1.5G  496K  1.5G   1% /run
/dev/sda2        20G  1.6G   19G   8% /
tmpfs           1.5G   84K  1.5G   1% /dev/shm
tmpfs           4.0M     0  4.0M   0% /sys/fs/cgroup
tmpfs           1.5G     0  1.5G   0% /tmp
tmpfs           299M     0  299M   0% /run/user/0
```

Also, other commands work. For example:

```
[root@archlinux ~]# podman pull registry.access.redhat.com/ubi8/ubi-minimal
Trying to pull registry.access.redhat.com/ubi8/ubi-minimal:latest...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 76b9354adec6 done
Copying blob a591faa84ab0 done
Copying config dc080723f5 done
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
dc080723f596f2407300cca2c19a17accad89edcf39f7b8b33e6472dd41e30f1
[root@archlinux ~]# podman images
REPOSITORY                                   TAG     IMAGE ID      CREATED     SIZE
registry.access.redhat.com/ubi8/ubi-minimal  latest  dc080723f596  9 days ago  104 MB
[root@archlinux ~]# podman ps -a
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                                               COMMAND  CREATED         STATUS             PORTS   NAMES
f2913aad0eb9  registry.access.redhat.com/ubi8/ubi-minimal:latest  bash     49 seconds ago  Created                    beautiful_black
e0682160a6a2  registry.access.redhat.com/ubi8/ubi-minimal:latest  bash     32 seconds ago  Up 27 seconds ago          heuristic_sinoussi
[root@archlinux ~]
```

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
[root@archlinux ~]# podman version
Version:      3.0.0
API Version:  3.0.0
Go Version:   go1.15.8
Git Commit:   5b2585f5e91ca148f068cefa647c23f8b1ade622
Built:        Thu Feb 11 22:22:46 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
[root@archlinux ~]# podman info --debug
host:
  arch: amd64
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
[root@archlinux ~]# pacman -Qi podman

Version         : 3.0.0-1

                  libgpgme.so=11-64
Optional Deps   : podman-docker: for Docker-compatible CLI
                  btrfs-progs: support btrfs backend devices [installed]
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide?**

Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

Host: OS X Big Sur 
Guest VMs: Archlinux or RHEL8/CentOS8


I use QEMU to spin up guest VM on OS X host. This may very well be related to QEMU/libslirp. So, I opened following issues on those boards:

https://bugs.launchpad.net/qemu/+bug/1916344
https://gitlab.freedesktop.org/slirp/libslirp/-/issues/35

I am not sure where this problem is coming from exactly. Is it `podman` issue, or `qemu` issue or `libslirp` issue or `mac os x` issue? I don't know.

I know `podman` uses [slirp4netns](https://github.com/rootless-containers/slirp4netns) underneath. So, perhaps it's worth checking with that team as well.

is it easily reproducible?

Is only slirp supported for the networking?  Can you change the network driver?
I don't think this is a `podman` issue. This looks more like a `qemu/libslirp` issue. I will deal with it separately with those folks. Closing this for now.
